businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and enterprise AI data analytics platform leader, operating on three pillars: Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise. FY2025 revenue reached $4.48B (+56%), with U.S. commercial revenue surpassing $500M, marking successful civilian market expansion. The U.S. Navy's ShipOS project recently expanded with Keel joining, extending defense AI applications to submarine production. CTO Shyam Sankar stated that 'the U.S. has only 8 days of weapons for a major fight against China and needs 800,' underscoring the urgency of defense AI investment.

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Over 10x the S&P 500 avg (22x). Extreme premium even vs software avg (35x)

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS $1.34. Large growth premium but still elevated

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 $4.48B, FY2026E $7.41B (+65.5%). Growth is actually accelerating

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top-tier among SaaS companies. High profitability alongside rapid growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: FCF $2.1B. Best-in-class cash generation in software

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Backlog
keyMetrics.interpretation: Per Motley Fool. Multi-year revenue visibility secured

growthDrivers.title: Navy ShipOS $448M: AI Expands to Submarine Production
growthDrivers.description: Keel Holdings joined Palantir and the U.S. Navy on the ShipOS initiative. $448M authorized funding deploys Foundry and AIP across naval shipyards, submarine construction, and 100+ suppliers. Defense AI extending beyond the battlefield to manufacturing.

growthDrivers.title: UBS Raises Target to $200, Maintains Buy
growthDrivers.description: UBS analyst Karl Keirstead raised the target from $180 to $200 (+11%). Cites accelerating growth and AIP adoption as key drivers.

growthDrivers.title: CTO Sankar: '8 Days vs 800 Days' Defense AI Imperative
growthDrivers.description: CTO Shyam Sankar stated the U.S. has 'lost deterrence' with only 8 days of weapons for a China conflict and needs 800. Powerful narrative for defense AI budget expansion.

growthDrivers.title: $11.2B Revenue Backlog + $10B Army + $20B Anduril Subcontract
growthDrivers.description: Motley Fool analysis: $11.2B backlog, $10B direct Army contract, and Anduril $20B subcontract potential. Multi-year pipeline is extremely robust.

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 Revenue Outlook $7.41B (+65.5%), Growth Accelerating
growthDrivers.description: From 56% in FY2025 to 65.5% in FY2026. EPS expected to grow 113%.

growthDrivers.title: NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS Reference Architecture
growthDrivers.description: Palantir software as the standard OS on NVIDIA hardware. Positioned to capture sovereign AI infrastructure markets globally.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government/defense AI track record. Security clearance creates insurmountable barriers. ShipOS expands AI applications to military manufacturing.

moats.description: Highest-level DoD and intelligence community clearances. Competitors cannot enter.
AI deployed in submarine production, naval shipyards. Expanding beyond battlefield to manufacturing.
Sovereign AI OS + NVIDIA hardware ecosystem. Positioned as the standard for national AI infrastructure.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform. General-purpose vs Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data lakehouse expanding into analytics platform territory

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI. Trailing Palantir in scale and execution

recentNews.headline: PLTR drops 1.5% to $152.77 despite ShipOS expansion news
recentNews.significance: Navy ShipOS positive but broader market weakness dragged stock down

recentNews.headline: UBS raises target from $180 to $200, maintains Buy
recentNews.significance: Karl Keirstead cites accelerating growth and AIP expansion as basis for 11% target increase

recentNews.headline: CTO Sankar: 'U.S. has only 8 days of weapons for China war, needs 800'
recentNews.significance: Emphasizes urgency of defense AI investment. Strengthens Palantir's defense revenue narrative

recentNews.headline: Keel joins Palantir and U.S. Navy on ShipOS initiative ($448M)
recentNews.significance: AI deployment for submarine production acceleration. Foundry/AIP across 100+ suppliers

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: $11.2B backlog, $10B Army deal, 61% growth
recentNews.significance: Comprehensive analysis of multi-year pipeline and growth acceleration

analystOpinions.notableComment: UBS Karl Keirstead raised to $200 (Buy). Wedbush Dan Ives maintains $230. MarketBeat consensus $194.05. 16 of 28 rate Buy.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation
risks.description: P/E 221x is 10x the S&P 500 average. Any growth miss could trigger sharp decline.

risks.title: Insider Selling Continues
risks.description: CEO Karp and insiders selling ~$6M daily. 10b5-1 plan but raises trust questions.

risks.title: Government Revenue Concentration
risks.description: Significant portion of revenue from U.S. government contracts. Political shifts or budget cuts pose direct risk.

risks.title: Stock Dilution
risks.description: ~5% annual dilution from SBC. Ongoing shareholder value erosion.

risks.title: AI Market Competition
risks.description: Microsoft, AWS, Google expanding enterprise AI investments. Long-term market share defense required.

buyReasons.title: ShipOS $448M: AI Reaches Military Manufacturing
buyReasons.rationale: AI deployed in submarine production, naval shipyards, 100+ suppliers. Defense AI expanding beyond the battlefield.

buyReasons.title: $11.2B Backlog + $10B Army + $20B Anduril
buyReasons.rationale: Extremely robust pipeline providing multi-year growth visibility.

buyReasons.title: FY2026 Revenue +65.5%, Growth Accelerating
buyReasons.rationale: From 56% to 65.5%. EPS +113%. Earnings are catching up to the valuation at pace.

buyReasons.title: UBS $200 Upgrade, Institutional Interest Growing
buyReasons.rationale: UBS raised target 11%. Consensus $194 implies 27% upside from current levels.

overallOpinion: Keel joining the Navy ShipOS project extends Palantir's defense AI from the battlefield to submarine manufacturing. The $448M program deploys across 100+ suppliers. CTO Sankar's '8 days vs 800 days' statement underscores the urgency of defense AI investment.

UBS raised its target to $200 and the $11.2B backlog with 65.5% revenue growth outlook is solid. But despite ShipOS news, the stock fell 1.5%. At 221x P/E, good news alone isn't enough to move the needle.

Sitting just below the 50-day MA ($158) with RSI at 46, the stock is in neutral territory. Down 26% from the $207 high. The valuation makes lump-sum buying uncomfortable, so dollar-cost averaging in the $140-150 range is the prudent approach.

investmentVerdict.summary: ShipOS expansion, $11.2B backlog, and 65.5% growth acceleration are compelling. UBS $200 upgrade adds conviction. But at 221x P/E, dollar-cost averaging into the $140-150 zone is recommended over buying at current levels.

threads.hook: Palantir CTO: '8 days of weapons won't deter China'
threads.text:
$PLTR (Mar 19) $152.77 (-1.5%)

CTO Sankar: 'We have 8 days of weapons for a major fight against China. That's not deterrence. You need 800 days.' Keel joined the Navy ShipOS project, deploying AI across submarine production ($448M).

UBS raised its target to $200. $11.2B backlog with 65.5% growth outlook. Yet the stock dropped 1.5% on the day. At 221x P/E, even great news gets a shrug.

Consensus $194 (+27%). The $140-150 range offers a more comfortable entry for dollar-cost averaging.

- RSI 46 (neutral)

Consensus $194.05 (+27%)
Verdict: Buy (DCA, 6mo-1yr)

witty.hook: Palantir put AI in submarine factories. The stock went underwater too.
witty.text:
$PLTR (Mar 19) $152.77 (-1.5%)

CTO says '8 days of weapons.' The irony: PLTR holders have felt like they've been in a war zone for 26% from highs. ShipOS brings AI to submarine production but the stock submerged anyway.

UBS bumped to $200. CEO bumps $6M of stock sales per day. $11.2B backlog, 65.5% growth. The business writes comedy and tragedy simultaneously.

221x P/E means every day is an adventure. $140 is where the ride gets interesting.

empathy.hook: To Palantir holders watching great news and red candles
empathy.text:
$PLTR (Mar 19) $152.77 (-1.5%)

ShipOS expansion, UBS upgrade, CTO's powerful defense message. The headlines couldn't be better, yet the stock dropped again. At 221x P/E, this is the emotional tax you pay.

But the business is at its strongest ever. $11.2B backlog, 65.5% growth, 47% FCF margin. The fundamentals are catching up to the valuation.

Down 26% from highs. If you're long-term, these days test your conviction but don't change the thesis. Dollar-cost averaging smooths the ride.
